CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE OF JUL-22-2015 IS OFF CALENDAR - TRANSFERRED. NOTICE SENT BY COURT.

LAW AND MOTION 302, DEFENDANTS JIMMY FOUST, DESIREE AMOR, SHILOH FOUST, ALMA MATER, INC., AND VOL-TEN CORPORATION, INC.'S MOTION TO TRANSFER VENUE IS GRANTED. THE CASE IS TRANSFERRED TO SAN JOAQUIN COUNTY WHERE ALL OF THE DEFENDANTS RESIDE. PLAINTIFF CANNOT ESTABLISH SAN FRANCISCO VENUE BASED ON THE CONTRACT CLAIM. ONLY MR. FAUST IS A PARTY TO THAT CONTRACT AND THE CO-DEFENDANTS ARE NOT. THE ALTER EGO ALLEGATIONS DO NOT CHANGE THE ANALYSIS BECAUSE THE CO-DEFENDANTS CANNOT BE THE ALTER EGO OF AN INDIVIDUAL (MR. FOUST). THE PERSONAL INJURY EXCEPTION DOES NOT APPLY. DEFENDANTS CORRECTLY CITE CARRUTH V. SUP. CRT. (1978) 80 CAL.APP.3D 215, 219-220. THIS CASE DOES NOT ALLEGE PHYSICAL INJURY OR PHYSICAL PROPERTY DAMAGE. THE REQUEST FOR FEES IS DENIED. ORDER SIGNED. JUDGE: ERNEST H. GOLDSMITH; CLERK: SEAN KANE; REPORTER: MELANIE GHENO, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)

calendar.matters:
CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Jul-06-2015 Off Calendar

Notice Of Defendants Motion To Transfer Venue
Off Calendar May-18-2015 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES:

No appearances made in this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling. DEFENDANTS JIMMY FOUST, DESIREE AMOR, SHILOH FOUST, ALMA MATER, INC., AND VOL-TEN CORPORATION, INC.'S Motion To Transfer Venue is granted. The case is transferred to San Joaquin County where all of the defendants reside. Plaintiff cannot establish San Francisco venue based on the contract claim. Only Mr. Faust is a party to that contract and the co-defendants are not. The alter ego allegations do not change the analysis because the co-defendants cannot be the alter ego of an individual (Mr. Foust). The personal injury exception does not apply. Defendants correctly cite Carruth v. Sup. Crt. (1978) 80 Cal.App.3d 215, 219-220. This case does not allege physical injury or physical property damage. The request for fees is denied. ORDER SIGNED. Judge: Ernest H. Goldsmith; Clerk: Sean Kane; Reporter: Melanie Gheno, CSR #7489 (302/EHG)
